rule_candidates.normalized_text:
General Community Property Principles
•
Marital economic community begins upon marriage and ends upon divorce, death, or
permanent physical separation
•
community property: Acquired during marriage
•
separate property:
•
Earned before marriage, during separation, or following divorce
•
Inheritances, gifts, devises, bequests acquired during marriage
•
quasi-community property: Acquired by spouse/couple while domiciled in non-community property state that would have been community property if
acquired while domiciled in CA
rule_candidates.raw_text:
General Community Property Principles
•
Marital economic community begins upon marriage and ends upon divorce, death, or
permanent physical separation
•
CP: Acquired during marriage
•
SP:
•
Earned before marriage, during separation, or following divorce
•
Inheritances, gifts, devises, bequests acquired during marriage
•
QCP: Acquired by spouse/couple while domiciled in non-CP state that would have been CP if
acquired while domiciled in CA

of marriage × rate of return × years married)

rule_candidates.normalized_text:
Cause of Action Against Third Party
•
cause of action arose during marriage: Recovery is community property
•
Upon divorce: Recovery awarded to injured spouses unless (i) commingled funds or (ii) share
awarded to non-injured spouse in equity
•
cause of action arose before or after marriage: Recovery is separate property
rule_candidates.raw_text:
Cause of Action Against Third Party
•
COA arose during marriage: Recovery is CP
•
Upon divorce: Recovery awarded to injured spouses unless (i) commingled funds or (ii) share
awarded to non-injured spouse in equity
•
COA arose before or after marriage: Recovery is SP

children.title:

•

Cause of Action Against Spouse
•
Personal injury recovery is injured spouse’s SP
